sources.name: Castlevania Speedrunning Wiki — Cv4/tricks
sources.notes: Documents jump heights and frame counts (3-frame=45px/40f, 2-frame=41px/38f, 1-frame=36px/36f), whip animation timing (12 frames to hit, 12 more active), invincibility 80 frames, weapon damage values.

sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — SNES / Super Castlevania IV
sources.notes: Whip cancel timing, ring swing dynamics, ledge fall behavior. Qualitative mechanics, limited quantified values.

notes: Super Castlevania IV is a COMPLETE REDESIGN of Castlevania physics compared to NES CV1.
NES CV1: fixed jump trajectory (lookup table), no air control, 1px/f walk. SCV4: variable jump, full air control, faster movement.
The 8-directional whip is the signature new mechanic — Simon can whip in all 8 directions and grapple rings to swing.
Jump physics are VARIABLE (not lookup table) — this is the key departure from NES Castlevania design.
Gravity and initial jump velocity calculated from documented jump heights/frames.
Walk speed not explicitly documented in reviewed sources — estimated from community gameplay documentation.

nes_castlevania_comparison.jump_type: NES: fixed lookup table. SCV4: variable height simulation
nes_castlevania_comparison.whip_grapple: NES: none. SCV4: ring grapple for swinging
nes_castlevania_comparison.whip_timing: NES: 22f animation, hits at 17. SCV4: 12f to damage, 12f active (24f total)
nes_castlevania_comparison.stair_control: NES: locked while on stairs. SCV4: can jump on stairs

collision_grid.notes: CV4 uses 8×8 pixel collision tiles (half the visual tile size). The gaps every 8 pixels are exploitable in speedrunning.

walk_speed.notes: Walk speed not explicitly documented in reviewed sources. Community description: Simon moves 'quicker' than NES Castlevania (1.0 px/f). Estimated ~1.5 px/f. VERIFY against ROM.

ground_movement.notes: Movement appears to be instant velocity like NES Castlevania. No acceleration ramp.

jump._note: CRITICAL DIFFERENCE FROM NES CASTLEVANIA: SCV4 jump is variable height with full air control. NOT a lookup table.

calculated_physics._method: Derived from 3-frame jump: height=45px, airborne=40f, apex at frame 20. Using h=v₀t - ½gt²: at apex v=0, so v₀=g×20; height: 200g=45; g=0.225, v₀=4.5

initial_y_velocity_calculated.notes: Calculated from jump height/duration data. Assumes constant gravity. VERIFY against ROM.

gravity_calculated.notes: Calculated from jump data. Close to NES Castlevania's effective gravity. VERIFY.

notes: FULL AIR CONTROL — Simon can freely change horizontal direction mid-jump. Opposite of NES Castlevania.
Variable height: holding jump longer produces higher apex. NES Castlevania had fixed apex.
Can jump while on stairs — impossible in NES Castlevania.
The combination (variable height + full air control) makes SCV4 Simon the most mobile Castlevania protagonist to this point.

animation_timing.notes: Compare to NES CV: 22 total frames, hits at frame 17. SCV4 whip activates FASTER (12 vs 17 frames) and has a wider damage window (12 active vs ~5 in NES CV).

directions.description: Whip can be aimed in 8 directions: up, upper-right, right, lower-right (airborne), down (airborne), lower-left (airborne), left, upper-left
directions.notes: 8-directional whip is SCV4's signature mechanic. In NES CV1, whip only fires horizontal. The 8 directions transform whip from a fixed-horizontal tool to a flexible combat system.

ring_grapple.description: Simon can use the whip to grapple on designated ring points in the environment, swinging in a pendulum arc.
ring_grapple.mechanics: Attach to ring → swing using momentum → jump off at desired point
ring_grapple.swing_behavior: Pendulum physics. Swing height is higher when Simon's sprite faces right (TASVideos: 'oddly enough, this boost is higher when his sprite points to the right').
ring_grapple.notes: Ring grapple sections require swinging to build height/momentum before releasing at the optimal point. This is a precision timing challenge.

cross_boomerang.bonus: effectively double due to forward/backward hit on same throw
cross_boomerang.notes: Cross hits on both outgoing and return trajectories, making it highest total damage subweapon.

cooldown_after_hit.notes: 17-frame window after subweapon contacts enemy or candle where further hits are disabled.

invincibility_frames.notes: 80 frames after taking damage. Compare to NES CV1: 96 frames. Slightly shorter in SCV4.

damage_boost.description: Taking damage increases Simon's movement speed temporarily.
damage_boost.notes: Damage boost is exploited in speedrunning for faster horizontal movement. The invincibility frames + speed boost create a useful tool despite the HP cost.

simon_standing.notes: SCV4 uses 8×8 collision tiles. Simon's hitbox is multiple tiles wide and tall. Exact pixel dimensions need ROM verification.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: SCV4 represents the 'reboot' philosophy: take a beloved NES game's theme and completely rethink the mechanics for new hardware capabilities. The soul (castles, whip, subweapons, enemies) is the same; the physics are fundamentally different.

The 8-directional whip transforms the game from 'advance left-to-right timing the whip' to 'manage space in all directions.' Level design in SCV4 exploits the 8 directions — enemies attack from diagonals and above/below in ways NES CV couldn't.

Full air control + variable jump is the biggest quality-of-life improvement. NES Castlevania's locked-in jumps felt punishing. SCV4 Simon feels responsive. This changes the difficulty model from 'memorize exact jump points' to 'react to what's happening.'

The ring grapple sections create a movement mode shift mid-level — swinging on rings is a different physical challenge from walking and jumping. The pendulum physics create their own timing challenges.
The 8×8 collision tile grid creates a different 'feel' than the visual 16×16 tiles — smaller collision precision allows more nuanced interactions with terrain edges.
